title: atriplex prostrata subsp. calotheca
intro: Atriplex prostrata subsp. calotheca, also known as low saltbush or coastal saltbush, is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the family Amaranthaceae. It is native to the coastal regions of Australia, and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The plant has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, and has been shown to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Atriplex prostrata subsp. calotheca can be prepared into herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
